text: The cultists had a philosophy.

The void transcends reality, and by embracing
 itΓÇª so could they.
After all, this world was gone.
If they devoted themselves to despairΓÇª Would it welcome them?
A form of hope. Desperate.
Worthless in the end.
After eons alone, three again become one.
On the precipice of a broken world, there is hope.
The Epoch is reforged.
As the Traveler leftΓÇª
Their real journey began.
And that should do it!
This waypointΓÇÖs harder than I anticipated. But no worries, all good now.
Just in time. Looks like weΓÇÖve got company.
Anyway, off you go.
IΓÇÖll see you on the other side!
YouΓÇª pathetic, human, mistake.
You think you can kill me?
You think you can-
Enough!
Come, use the lance!
The Lance of Heorot. Where is it?
Quickly, you need to get out of here!
The lance was found, and a bond was formed.
Do you think weΓÇÖll see Heorot? Before...
Their fates intertwinedΓÇª
LetΓÇÖs go together.
With mine.
But, for now...
They focused on their gods.
The Traveler set sail...
Eager to make it to the Isle of Storms.
The ship is secure! Should reach the pier in no time.
LagonΓÇÖs temple, a beacon of light.
Careful. LagonΓÇÖs not used to visitors.
The Isle of Storms, complete with its capricious god.
You think this is over?
You know nothing of the power I've been given.
Orobyss showed me the way... all I needed was the key.
And you brought it right to me!
Do you feel it, Traveler?
The shackles of fate, binding tighter...
Apophis slipping beyond your reach...
Your fate is setΓÇª and it always ends with me.
Come. See for yourself.
Inside the Monolith.
There are other realities, that fought against their fate.
But no matter their sacrifice...
They fell.
Champions. Empires. Gods.
All in the end... aberrations.
There is no escape from the Void.
In the beginning, there was darkness.
Only Eterra... myself... and the Void.
I was content with nothing.
She dreamt of light, of lifeΓÇª of humanity.
We disagreed.
And yet, in her world...
Majasa of the desertΓÇª Lagon of the Sea.
Rahyeh, of the endless SkiesΓÇª
and Heorot, of the frozen Tundra.
But fate stole Eterra away.
Without her, there was conflict.
Then, wars.
In the end, it was Rahyeh, who sought to outshine the sun...
That would pull the world into his flame.
Caught within RahyehΓÇÖs gaze, were the Keepers.
An order sworn to protect EterraΓÇÖs memoryΓÇªand the power it still holds.
TimeΓÇª was running out.
Heorot offered the Keepers his strength, sending champions to war.
They were joined by someone unexpected.
A Traveler. Willing to walk into the flames of war.
But why?
Was it honor they sought? Power? Redemption?
Or was this fate?
Perhaps.
But no story is quite that simple...
There were Gods.
The power of the Epoch is mine!
There is no escape... from me.
Orobyss used me... I deserve this fate.
What? Traveler...
Their story will not end here, I will make sure of that.
The past has nothing left to offer.
Fate is in motion now, its darkness can not be denied.
But fate can be delayed, even if for a moment.
That... will be my...
Final gift.
